# UC-23.6.2 · Expense Report Anomaly Detection

## Description

Flags SAP Concur expense entry lines ingested into Splunk where rule-driven anomaly codes attach — Benford-adjacent round-dollar thresholds common to split-limit gaming, corridor amounts parked just below receipt or successive approval tiers, Saturday-or-Sunday discretionary expense-type postings when paired with audit-sensitive categories, missing receipt imagery beyond typical IRS-style substantiation floors when dollar amounts exceed twenty-five currency units, merchant-description keyword hits aligned to prohibited-spend categories, and two-sigma upward outliers versus peer averages inside each department-by-expense-type cohort — then aggregates flagged dollar exposure by submitting employee login so Finance receives a reimbursement-exception queue rather than reading full populations.

## Value

Accounts-payable and internal-audit teams concentrate scarce review hours on employee reimbursement outliers aligned with occupational-fraud statistics — ACFE Report to the Nations highlights travel-and-entertainment reimbursement schemes among frequent asset-misappropriation patterns with median fraud-loss figures material enough to impair earnings narratives — while COSO-style segregation-of-duties monitoring and Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 expectations both reward continuous transaction surveilling rather than sampling alone; Splunk operationalizes those signals across SaaS expense SaaS feeds plus ERP JDBC corroboration so policy owners defend reimbursement controls before quarter attestations without treating customer collections dashboards that UC-23.6.1 targets.

## Implementation

Stand up OAuth-backed Concur polling into `index=business` / `concur:expense:entries`, bind HR `department`, enable HEC for Expensify webhooks where deployed, add DB Connect JDBC slices over AP mart tables for duplicate-payment joins, normalize currency columns before thresholds, save this SPL as `finance_te_anomaly_review_queue`, schedule weekly or nightly over `-90d`, route CSV digest to Controller mailbox rather than Sev1 paging.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier aligned with splunkVersions; Heavy Forwarder or container worker hosting Splunk DB Connect alongside outbound HTTPS allowances toward SAP Concur API endpoints plus JDBC connectivity into Oracle AP reporting schemas or SAP-supported warehouse replicas Finance sanctions.
• Splunk roles restricting reimbursement anomaly outputs to Finance investigations, Internal Audit, and Controller delegates — avoid surfacing HR disciplinary conclusions solely from Splunk dashboards.
• Corporate travel policy artifact keyed by effective dates (`policy_version`, receipt thresholds, FX rounding tables) maintained outside Splunk yet referenced via lookups referenced inside SPL macros.
• Splunk DB Connect identities granted read-only JDBC across curated expense mart projections (`vw_ap_expense_lines`) tied to ERP posting batches employees ultimately recognize as reimbursement cash movements.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install Splunk DB Connect from Splunkbase 2686 on the Heavy Forwarder bundle hosting JDBC workloads; register Oracle JDBC (`ojdbc11.jar`) or SAP HANA (`ngdbc.jar`) drivers consistent with ERP vendor contracts.

Provision OAuth credentials inside Secret Storage or Splunk credential overlays bound to custom modular inputs polling SAP Concur Expense APIs — authenticate against regional hosts (`POST /oauth2/v0/token`) capturing scopes covering expense reporting (`EXP_RPT`) plus receipt retrieval (`RECEIPTS`). Implement exponential backoff honoring Concur thirty-requests-per-minute guidance documented by SAP developer outreach.

Emit normalized JSON events into `index=business` sourcetype `concur:expense:entries`, stamping `_time` from `TransactionDate` where authoritative rather than indexer receipt latency.

Stand complementary JDBC inputs emitting `dbx:ap_expense_lines` rows keyed by cleared ERP voucher identifiers (`belnr`, `buzei`, Oracle `invoice_id`) so duplicate-detection SPL extensions join SaaS merchant descriptions against cleared postings.

Enable Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens scoped for Expensify Integration Server webhook deliveries (`expensify.report.state.change`) landing sourcetype `expensify:report`, forwarding TLS-terminated payloads through Splunk Cloud ACS routing rules where Finance forbids Heavy Forwarder hops.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL from the parent `spl` field into Search & Reporting; macro-wrap threshold literals (`threshold_low_receipt`, `threshold_vp_cap`) inside `macros.conf` so Corporate Accounting adjusts corridor widths without rewriting SPL fragments.

Save as scheduled report `finance_te_anomaly_weekly`; attach alert actions emailing CSV extracts through scripted alerts — throttle per `OwnerLoginID` across seventy-two hours because benign travelers accumulate repetitive grocery receipts flagged multiple nights.

Step 3 — Mechanics auditors care about
Concur expense entries arrive pre-exploded into vendor-facing dimensions Finance recognizes (`ExpenseTypeCode`, `ReceiptImageStatus`), letting Splunk attach deterministic anomaly codes without rebuilding OCR pipelines locally.

`transaction_dow` interprets ISO weekday digits Monday-through-Sunday (`strftime(... "%u")`) so Saturday equals six — discretionary categories layered atop weekends behave distinctly from weekday commuter meals sharing identical merchants.

`eventstats` calculates cohort averages strictly inside `(department, ExpenseTypeCode)` buckets — tightening cohort scope avoids comparing airfare magnitudes against taxi vouchers absent HR diligence.

The trailing `stats` block collapses noisy row-level ledger noise into reimbursement-owner summaries suited for managerial escalation workflows synchronized with HR confidentiality norms.

Step 4 — Operationalize and validate
Publish a Dashboard Studio workbook (`Finance_TE_Anomaly_Review`) pinned to Controller and Travel Risk stewards with row-level CSV export restricted via Splunk role maps — default cadence remains weekly Monday reviews rather than paging duty rotations.

Route scheduled-report alert actions delivering gzip CSV attachments through authenticated SMTP relays into the shared mailbox finance-te-review@example.com with retention aligned to HR investigation policies rather than immutable SIEM buckets.

Maintain `te_review_disposition.csv` KV lookups edited only by audit managers so downstream Sankey panels join anomaly codes to disposition outcomes without rewriting SPL skeletons weekly.

Pick five flagged rows inside Splunk; locate identical expense artifacts inside SAP Concur Expense workspace URLs referenced through deep-link macros embedding `ReportID` plus `EntryID`.

Cross-foot summed flagged reimbursement totals versus Controller-certified expense-aging extracts pulled straight from ERP AP modules DB Connect mirrors — tolerate rounding deltas smaller than ledger posting tolerance documented under corporate accounting policies.

Compare anomaly percentages versus Internal Audit sampling schedules referenced during Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 testing narratives.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• SAP Concur Insights Premium plus AppZen Mastermind frequently reroutes obviously abusive submissions inside vendor workflows before Splunk ingestion mirrors JSON payloads — downstream Splunk dashboards under-count headline fraud yet remain vital for residue-pattern hunting AppZen deliberately ignores.
• Expensify SmartScan OCR occasionally mistranscribes handwritten totals (`USD 50.00` versus `USD 49.99`), falsely triggering JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD until processors manually rewrite digits.
• Concur receipt-upload UX delays flipping `ReceiptImageStatus` from Missing to Received up to forty-eight hours while travelers sync mobile uploads — MISSING_RECEIPT_OVER_THRESHOLD clears automatically after nightly reconciliation reruns.
• Travel-heavy metro assignments legitimately crowd seventy-four-dollar meals beneath IRS-style receipt thresholds despite appearances of corridor stacking — layering geo lookups resolves conflicts absent blunt suppression.
• Currency pipelines mixing USD-approved totals against EUR-line postings exaggerate corridor breaches whenever treasury publishes averaged ECB rates asynchronously versus card-authorization timestamps Splunk snapshots capture.
• Statistical cohort counts (`dept_n`) smaller than twenty intentionally suppress variance math — rotational hires joining departments mid-quarter inherit unstable denominators until cohort maturity returns.
• Travel-policy revisions shifting seventy-five-dollar receipt floors upward mid-quarter orphan legacy Splunk thresholds referencing stale macros unless governance publishes simultaneous macro edits tied to effective dating controls.
• Employees splitting cost-center allocations across departments distort peer-average integrity unless Splunk adopts weighted-average attribution sourced from HR hierarchy feeds refreshed nightly.
• AI-centric vendors removing obviously fraudulent receipts upstream shrink Splunk-visible severity distributions yet amplify residual long-tail ambiguity investigators still adjudicate manually.
• Quarterly Sarbanes-Oxley-testing mandates auditing every reimbursement packet briefly overwhelming Splunk-derived queues despite dashboards emphasizing statistically prioritized subsets.
• Splunk DB Connect JDBC pulls capped below vendor-imposed fetch-row ceilings occasionally truncate intra-day batches — reconcile watermark checkpoints whenever Oracle listeners emit fetch-aborted warnings referencing incomplete vouchers.

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="concur:expense:entries" earliest=-90d
| eval TransactionDate_clean=replace(coalesce(TransactionDate,transaction_date,""),"Z","")
| eval td_epoch=coalesce(strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"),strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval transaction_dow=if(isnotnull(td_epoch),tonumber(strftime(td_epoch,"%u")),null())
| eval amount=tonumber(TransactionAmount)
| eval department=tostring(coalesce(department,DepartmentName,OrgUnit,"UNKNOWN"))
| eval anomaly_flags=mvappend(
    if(amount=round(amount,0) AND amount>=100 AND (amount=100 OR amount=200 OR amount=250 OR amount=500 OR amount=1000 OR amount=2500 OR amount=5000), "ROUND_NUMBER_SPLIT_RULE", null()),
    if((amount>=24 AND amount<25) OR (amount>=74 AND amount<75) OR (amount>=499 AND amount<500) OR (amount>=2499 AND amount<2500), "JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD", null()),
    if(transaction_dow>=6 AND ExpenseTypeCode IN ("ENT","MEAL","TRVL"), "WEEKEND_DISCRETIONARY_CATEGORY", null()),
    if(ReceiptImageStatus IN ("Missing","NotReceived") AND amount>25, "MISSING_RECEIPT_OVER_THRESHOLD", null()),
    if(match(VendorDescription,"(?i)(liquor|casino|adult|sportsbook|cannabis)"), "OUT_OF_POLICY_VENDOR", null()))
| eventstats avg(amount) as dept_avg, stdev(amount) as dept_stdev, count as dept_n by department, ExpenseTypeCode
| eval statistical_outlier=if(dept_n>=20 AND amount > dept_avg + 2*dept_stdev, "STATISTICAL_OUTLIER_2SIGMA", null())
| eval anomaly_flags=mvappend(anomaly_flags, statistical_outlier)
| where mvcount(anomaly_flags) > 0
| stats count as flagged_entries, sum(amount) as total_flagged_amount, values(anomaly_flags) as reasons, values(VendorDescription) as merchants by OwnerLoginID, department
| eventstats sum(total_flagged_amount) as portfolio_flagged
| eval pct_of_portfolio=round(100*total_flagged_amount/portfolio_flagged, 1)
| sort - total_flagged_amount
| table OwnerLoginID, department, flagged_entries, total_flagged_amount, pct_of_portfolio, reasons, merchants
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio table ranked by `total_flagged_amount` showing `OwnerLoginID`, `department`, `pct_of_portfolio`, and multi-value `reasons` (top-flagged employees triage queue); stacked column chart counting anomaly-flag hits stacked by decoded reason codes across the fleet (ROUND_NUMBER_SPLIT_RULE versus JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD breakdown); matrix-style heatmap faceting anomaly reason against `department` for hotspot geography; single-value visualization pair charting percentage of reimbursed dollars touching any anomaly flag plus twenty-eight-day sparkline trend from summary-index acceleration of the nightly run; optional Sankey diagram flowing anomaly-type buckets into disposition buckets (`confirmed_policy_exception`, `escalated_hotline`, `cleared_false_positive`) sourced from manual KV-store lookups Finance annotates after review.
